hero.title: Rise of the Ronin
hero.description: Explore Bakumatsu-era Japan as a custom ronin, master nine melee weapons and varied combat styles, build bonds, make story-changing choices, and complete missions solo or in online co-op.

gameFeature.title: Rise of the Ronin Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Master nine melee weapons, the Ten-Chi-Jin combat style system and Counterspark parries across an open-world Bakumatsu Japan, solo or in three-player online co-op.

beginnerGuide.title: Rise of the Ronin Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Rise of the Ronin begins with a linear prologue before opening into a large region filled with missions, fugitives, bandit bases and character bonds. Focus first on learning Ki management and Counterspark timing, then strengthen your character through exploration instead of rushing only through the main story.

steps.title: Choose the Right Difficulty
steps.description: Select the setting that matches how much combat pressure you want.
actions: Choose Dawn for stronger recovery items and lower Ki consumption from attacks.
Choose Dusk for the default balance between exploration and combat difficulty.
Choose Twilight for more aggressive enemies and much more dangerous attacks.
Change the difficulty later from the world map or before important story missions.
steps.result: Dawn is the safest starting choice for new action-RPG players, while Dusk suits players familiar with parry-based combat.

steps.title: Use the Prologue as Combat Training
steps.description: The opening section introduces movement, stealth, melee weapons, ranged tools and the first major encounters.
actions: Follow the tutorial prompts instead of immediately trying advanced combinations.
Test several available weapons and choose one whose attack rhythm feels predictable.
Review control and tutorial reminders from the menu whenever a mechanic is unclear.
Practice blocking and dodging before relying entirely on Countersparks.
steps.result: Finishing the prologue with a reliable defensive routine makes the first Yokohama encounters considerably easier.

steps.title: Learn Counterspark Timing
steps.description: Counterspark is both an attack and a parry, activated with Triangle on the default PS5 controls.
actions: Watch the enemy's weapon rather than reacting to the first body movement.
Press Counterspark shortly before the attack connects.
Use block or dodge when an enemy's combination is still unfamiliar.
Counterspark glowing red attacks only after learning their delayed or multi-hit timing.
steps.result: Successful Countersparks panic enemies, reduce their combat momentum and create openings for major Ki damage.

steps.title: Protect and Recover Your Ki
steps.description: Attacking, dodging, guarding and Martial Skills consume Ki, leaving you vulnerable when the gauge is empty.
actions: Avoid spending the entire Ki bar on one uninterrupted attack string.
Pause briefly after a safe combination instead of continuing until exhaustion.
Use Blade Flash after landing attacks to clean blood from the weapon and recover Ki.
Save Ki-heavy Martial Skills for panicked enemies or clear punish windows.
steps.result: Maintaining a Ki reserve allows you to block, dodge or Counterspark when an enemy suddenly retaliates.

steps.title: Carry Complementary Weapons
steps.description: Prepare a dependable melee weapon and a ranged sub-weapon rather than building around only one attack type.
actions: Use a katana, spear or similar balanced weapon while learning enemy patterns.
Keep a bow for quiet ranged attacks and isolated stealth kills.
Use firearms when you need immediate power or a timed shot that can stun an enemy.
Attach newly unlocked combat styles and test their Counterspark animations before important duels.
steps.result: A mixed loadout lets you weaken groups from range before entering a close-range fight.

steps.title: Follow a Safe Yokohama Route
steps.description: After the linear opening, strengthen nearby areas before pushing deep into major story missions.
actions: Clear smaller bandit-controlled locations near your current route.
Defeat nearby fugitives when their level is close to yours.
Complete local errands and short side missions for additional rewards.
Visit landmarks and collect nearby exploration objectives while travelling between missions.
steps.result: This route raises your level, supplies replacement equipment and improves local services without excessive grinding.

steps.title: Raise Region and Character Bonds
steps.description: Bonds are a core progression system rather than optional conversation content.
actions: Restore Public Order by clearing hostile bases and dangerous fugitives.
Complete requests for residents, merchants, monks and travelling characters.
Increase character bonds through missions, conversations and suitable gifts.
Prioritize bonds connected to the weapon styles you actively use.
steps.result: Region bonds provide benefits such as merchant discounts, while character bonds can unlock weapons, items, skills and combat styles.

steps.title: Manage Equipment Efficiently
steps.description: Early missions provide frequent weapon and armor replacements, so avoid overinvesting in temporary gear.
actions: Equip items that improve your current attack or defense without breaking an important set bonus.
Compare special effects instead of selecting equipment only by rarity.
Keep pieces that support Countersparks, Ki recovery, assassinations or your preferred weapon.
Sell or dismantle obsolete equipment and reserve upgrades for gear you expect to use across several missions.
steps.result: A smaller, focused inventory makes equipment comparisons faster and preserves upgrade materials.

steps.title: Use the Longhouse
steps.description: Your lodging becomes an important preparation area after reaching Yokohama.
actions: Speak with visiting companions and give gifts to improve bonds.
Adjust your appearance and organize equipment before major missions.
Reset skill points when your current allocation no longer matches your playstyle.
Prepare multiple weapon and style combinations for different enemy matchups.
steps.result: The ability to reset skills means an early allocation does not permanently lock your character into one build.

steps.title: Do Not Restart Over Story Choices
steps.description: Important decisions change missions and relationships, but alternative outcomes become accessible later.
actions: Continue with the choice that best matches your preferred faction or character.
Reach Edo to unlock the Testament of the Soul system.
Replay completed missions through Testament of the Soul.
Select different choices to view alternative mission routes and consequences.
steps.result: You can explore other outcomes without abandoning your original save or repeating the entire game.

quickTips: Ki management is the core of combat—never empty your gauge on a single combination.
Counterspark timing matters more than raw aggression; learn enemy wind-ups first.
Mix a melee weapon with a ranged sub-weapon to handle groups safely.
Strengthen nearby Yokohama areas before pushing deep into main story missions.

weaponsTierList.title: Rise of the Ronin Weapons Tier List
weaponsTierList.intro: Rise of the Ronin includes nine primary melee weapon categories with multiple combat styles, plus unarmed combat as a specialist option. The rankings below prioritize general story progression and boss consistency; every equipped weapon becomes much stronger when paired with the correct Ten, Chi or Jin style.

weapons.name: Katana
weapons.bestFor: Balanced progression, duels and players learning Counterspark timing
weapons.notes: The katana combines responsive attacks, strong Martial Skills and a large selection of combat styles. It has no major weakness and adapts to nearly every story or boss encounter.

weapons.name: Paired Swords
weapons.bestFor: Aggressive pressure, rapid style switching and mobile encounters
weapons.notes: Fast attack recovery makes it easy to stop a combination and defend. The short reach requires close positioning, but repeated attacks can rapidly pressure an enemy's Ki.

weapons.name: Oxtail Blade
weapons.bestFor: Fast movement, wide attacks and fighting multiple nearby enemies
weapons.notes: The flexible blade combines speed with unusually broad reach. Its flowing animations require practice, but it maintains pressure while repositioning around an opponent.

weapons.name: Polearm
weapons.bestFor: Safe spacing, crowd control and controlled aggression
weapons.notes: Long sweeping attacks control a large area and reduce the need to stand directly beside an enemy, making it dependable during exploration and multi-enemy encounters.

weapons.name: Odachi
weapons.bestFor: Heavy punish attacks, stance breaking and direct boss damage
weapons.notes: The odachi rewards deliberate timing with powerful health and Ki damage. Longer animations make missed attacks dangerous, so it performs best after a Counterspark or clear opening.

weapons.name: Spear
weapons.bestFor: Defensive spacing, precise thrusts and safe early progression
weapons.notes: The spear offers consistent reach and straightforward attacks without the slow recovery of the heaviest weapons, making it a reliable choice for learning enemy ranges.

weapons.name: Saber
weapons.bestFor: Mobile dueling, quick punish attacks and lightweight enemies
weapons.notes: The saber emphasizes speed and movement over single-hit power, working well for short attacks followed by an immediate block, dodge or Counterspark.

weapons.name: Bayonet
weapons.notes: The bayonet combines close-range thrusts with firearm actions to threaten enemies at different distances, though managing its full moveset is more demanding than a conventional blade.

weapons.name: Greatsword
weapons.bestFor: Maximum single-hit damage and experienced heavy-weapon players
weapons.notes: The greatsword can heavily punish broken or panicked enemies, but its slow attacks leave limited room for mistakes against fast bosses.

weapons.name: Unarmed
weapons.bestFor: Challenge runs, non-lethal flavor and close-range experimentation
weapons.notes: Unarmed combat is responsive but lacks the reach, equipment variety and style selection of normal weapons, so it is better treated as a specialist option.

combatStylesCounterspark.title: Rise of the Ronin Combat Styles and Counterspark Guide
combatStylesCounterspark.intro: Combat styles change a weapon's moveset, Martial Skills and Counterspark behavior. An advantageous style causes stronger stagger and Ki pressure, so successful players switch styles according to the enemy's weapon instead of remaining in one stance for every fight.

styles.name: Ten
styles.effectiveAgainst: Katanas and other normal or medium-weight weapons
styles.recommendedUse: Use Ten for common sword-wielding enemies and traditional katana duels.

styles.name: Chi
styles.effectiveAgainst: Odachis, greatswords and other heavy weapons
styles.weakAgainst: Katanas and other normal or medium-weight weapons
styles.recommendedUse: Use Chi to interrupt slower heavy attacks and apply stronger Ki pressure after a successful deflection.

styles.name: Jin
styles.recommendedUse: Use Jin against fast Western-style blades and opponents built around quick attack strings.

styles.name: Shinobi
styles.recommendedUse: Use Shinobi when you can consistently perform precisely timed Countersparks.

mechanics.name: Counterspark
mechanics.input: Triangle on the default PS5 controls
mechanics.function: Performs an attack that can simultaneously deflect an incoming enemy strike.
mechanics.bestTiming: Press shortly before impact, accounting for delayed attacks and the final hit of longer combinations.
mechanics.advancedTip: Countersparks can deflect glowing red attacks, but dodging is safer until their timing is familiar.

mechanics.name: Panic
mechanics.input: Triggered by effective Countersparks and combat pressure
mechanics.function: Temporarily disrupts the enemy and creates an opening for stronger attacks.
mechanics.bestTiming: Attack immediately after the successful Counterspark while the enemy cannot recover normally.
mechanics.advancedTip: Use the panic window to reduce maximum Ki before attempting to empty the remaining Ki gauge.

mechanics.name: Blade Flash
mechanics.function: Cleans blood from the weapon and restores Ki according to the accumulated blood gauge.
mechanics.bestTiming: Activate between combinations or immediately before returning to defense.
mechanics.advancedTip: Frequent Blade Flash use supports sustained aggression without leaving the Ki gauge empty.

mechanics.name: Martial Skills
mechanics.input: Activated through the equipped combat style
mechanics.function: Consumes Ki to perform a powerful move with properties unique to that style.
mechanics.bestTiming: Use after a Counterspark, during panic or when the enemy's maximum Ki has already been reduced.
mechanics.advancedTip: Some Martial Skills change when their input is held or repeatedly pressed.

mechanics.name: Ki Break and Critical Hit
mechanics.function: Breaking the enemy's stance leaves them unable to act and opens a Critical Hit opportunity.
mechanics.bestTiming: Reduce maximum Ki first, then use Martial Skills and effective styles to empty the remaining gauge.
mechanics.advancedTip: Against powerful enemies, maximum-Ki damage is often more important than repeatedly attacking health.

mechanics.name: Block and Dodge
mechanics.input: Use the standard guard and evasive controls
mechanics.function: Provides safer defensive alternatives when a Counterspark would be too risky.
mechanics.bestTiming: Block predictable normal strikes and dodge grabs, delayed attacks or combinations with uncertain timing.
mechanics.advancedTip: A mixed defense is more reliable than attempting to Counterspark every attack.

mechanics.name: Unlocking New Styles
mechanics.input: Story progress, difficult opponents and bonds
mechanics.function: Adds new movesets and Martial Skills to compatible weapon types.
mechanics.bestTiming: Raise bonds with characters and factions connected to the weapons you use most.
mechanics.advancedTip: Check each style's progression conditions because different styles evolve through different activities.

mechanics.name: Expanding Style Coverage
mechanics.input: Equip compatible styles through the Equipment menu
mechanics.function: Maintains Ten, Chi and Jin answers for different enemy weapon categories.
mechanics.bestTiming: Prepare all three standard matchups before a major mission or boss sequence.
mechanics.advancedTip: Equipping the same weapon type in both primary weapon slots can expand the available styles to a maximum of six.

bestBuildsSkillsGear.title: Rise of the Ronin Best Builds, Skills and Gear
bestBuildsSkillsGear.intro: Strength improves direct attack and defense, Dexterity supports secondary attacks and stagger, Charm improves resistance to Ki damage, Ki recovery and luck, while Intellect supports status effects and resistance. Skill points can be reset at the Longhouse, allowing you to change direction when a new weapon or combat style becomes available.

builds.name: Strength Aggressive Melee
builds.role: High direct damage and strong close-range punishment
skillPriorities: Primary-weapon attack and defensive improvements
Martial Skill damage and Ki damage
Guard efficiency and survival during close-range pressure
Damage or Ki recovery effects triggered after successful Countersparks
gearPriorities: High attack weapons with effects matching the equipped weapon type
Armor that improves defense, Ki management or close-range damage
Set bonuses that strengthen Martial Skills, Countersparks or direct melee attacks
Accessories with consistent combat effects rather than narrow exploration bonuses
builds.combatLoop: Counterspark or guard the opening attack, use a short normal combination, perform Blade Flash, then spend Ki on a Martial Skill while the enemy is panicked.

builds.name: Charm Parry and Ki-Break
builds.role: Stable Ki recovery, strong defense and repeated Counterspark pressure
skillPriorities: Ki recovery and resistance to enemy Ki damage
Counterspark-related recovery and follow-up pressure
Critical Hit damage after breaking an enemy's stance
Speechcraft and bond utility after core combat skills are secured
gearPriorities: Effects activated by successful Countersparks
Reduced Ki consumption while guarding or attacking
Improved Ki recovery during Blade Flash or defensive actions
Set bonuses that support long duels instead of only first-hit damage
builds.combatLoop: Identify the enemy weapon, switch to the advantageous style, Counterspark the final strike of the combination and attack during panic until a Critical Hit becomes available.

builds.name: Dexterity Stealth Assassin
builds.role: Fast eliminations, mobility and controlled entry into larger encounters
skillPriorities: Assassination and rapid stealth takedown improvements
Secondary-weapon damage and stagger
Movement, detection and ranged utility
Skills that improve follow-up attacks after an assassination
gearPriorities: Assassination damage and effects activated after stealth kills
Movement, Ki recovery and fast-weapon bonuses
Bow or shuriken effects for silent enemy removal
Accessories that support stealth, Dexterity or secondary weapons
builds.combatLoop: Mark isolated enemies, remove sentries with assassinations or silent ranged attacks, frighten nearby opponents and finish the remaining group with fast Ki pressure.

builds.name: Intellect Ranged Technician
builds.role: Ranged control, status application, medicine and flexible preparation
skillPriorities: Status infliction and resistance
Medicine, remedy and consumable improvements
Firearm, bow and secondary-weapon utility
Adaptability for difficult missions containing elemental or status threats
gearPriorities: Effects that improve ranged attacks, ammunition or status application
Weapon bonuses that activate after ranged hits
Armor that improves medicine effectiveness or status resistance
Accessories that strengthen Intellect, Dexterity or consumable use
builds.combatLoop: Open with a bow or firearm, apply pressure before enemies close the distance, switch to the correct melee style and use medicine or status tools during safe gaps.

builds.name: Balanced Ronin
builds.role: Flexible story progression with access to combat, exploration and dialogue tools
skillPriorities: Core healing, Ki and defensive improvements
At least one useful assassination upgrade
Basic ranged and consumable utility
Speechcraft options that create additional mission or dialogue solutions
gearPriorities: High-value equipment with broadly useful attack, defense and Ki effects
A complete set bonus that works in most encounters
One fast weapon and one weapon with greater reach
Accessories that cover missing defense, recovery or utility
builds.combatLoop: Begin at range when possible, switch to the advantageous Ten, Chi or Jin style, use a safe defensive response and spend Martial Skills only after creating a clear opening.

builds.name: Universal Gear Rules
builds.role: Efficient equipment management for every build
skillPriorities: Unlock essential survival and Ki tools before small percentage bonuses
Invest in skills that support actions used in every fight
Use bond rewards to unlock additional styles before judging a weapon
Reset skill points at the Longhouse when changing your main weapon
gearPriorities: Activate complete set bonuses instead of wearing several incomplete sets
Match weapon effects to the exact actions used by the build
Use accessories to add recovery, defense, utility or build-specific effects
Keep specialized pieces for stealth, ranged or boss loadouts
builds.combatLoop: Check the enemy weapon, equip an advantageous style, manage Ki with Blade Flash and convert a stance break into a Critical Hit.

pcRequirementsPerformanceUpdates.title: Rise of the Ronin PC Requirements and Performance
pcRequirementsPerformanceUpdates.intro: The Steam version requires a 64-bit Windows PC, 16 GB of RAM, DirectX 12 and 155 GB of available storage on an SSD. Its official targets are 1080p at 30 FPS on minimum hardware and 1080p at 60 FPS on recommended hardware, both using upscaling.

rows.minimum: Intel Core i5-10400 or AMD Ryzen 5 1600
rows.recommended: Intel Core i5-10600K or AMD Ryzen 5 5600X

rows.minimum: NVIDIA GTX 1060 6 GB or AMD RX 5500 XT 8 GB
rows.recommended: NVIDIA RTX 2080 Super 8 GB or AMD RX 6700 XT 12 GB
155 GB available space; NVMe SSD recommended

rows.result: 1080p at 30 FPS; Frame Generation can raise FPS further
1080p at 60 FPS; Frame Generation can raise FPS further

rows.settings: Intel Core i5-12400, Arc B580 12 GB, standard settings and upscaling

rows.details: 8K output is supported on suitable hardware and display configurations
The PC version includes a 120 FPS option

rows.support: AMD FSR, NVIDIA DLSS, Intel XeSS
rows.details: Multiple vendor upscalers are supported for higher performance
Frame Generation can be used to increase FPS
Reflex support is included for compatible NVIDIA hardware
Wide-aspect monitor support is built into the Steam version
Key bindings are customizable and menus support mouse clicking
The PC feature set includes DirectX 12 Ultimate support

rows.highlights: Fixed crashes caused by certain Windows updates when specific characters or equipment appeared on screen
Added save-data recovery for the bond mission In Search of New Knowledge and improved stuttering in specific areas
Added Dynamic Adjustment of FPS, reduced VRAM usage and improved GPU performance for ambient occlusion and upscaling
Added Prioritize Graphics and Prioritize FPS modes, optimized demanding settings and created an additional backup-save location
Adjusted graphics presets, added a Recommended preset and fixed launch crashes on CPUs with 33 or more threads

walkthroughMissionsBosses.title: Rise of the Ronin Walkthrough, Missions and Bosses
walkthroughMissionsBosses.intro: The campaign moves through three major open-world regions while faction choices, character bonds and optional missions change available allies and mission outcomes. Use side activities to strengthen region bonds before major story operations, then use Testament of the Soul to replay completed missions and explore alternate paths.

stages.region: Veiled Edge and the Black Ships
stages.storyFocus: Complete the linear opening, learn the core combat systems and begin the search that sends the ronin toward Yokohama.
keyMissions: Departure toward the first open-world region
preparation: Practice Counterspark timing against basic attack strings
Learn when to block or dodge instead of forcing a parry
Test melee and ranged weapons before committing upgrade resources
majorEncounters: Early duels that teach Ki pressure and critical hits
Black Ship opponents using both Japanese and Western weapons

stages.storyFocus: Meet pro-Shogunate, anti-Shogunate and Western figures while building the first major character and region bonds.
keyMissions: The first major story choice after the battle with Gonzo
Aerial base infiltration to recover Igashichi Iizuka's camera
preparation: Restore Public Order in nearby districts
Complete errands, fugitive hunts and Bond Missions
Raise bonds with companions before selecting mission allies
majorEncounters: Club-wielding brute during the camera recovery operation
unlocks: Gift giving and deeper companion interactions
More combat styles through story and bonds

stages.region: Edo Castle, Asakusa, Nihonbashi and Yoshiwara
stages.storyFocus: Enter the political center of the shogunate, take on higher-level faction missions and gain access to mission replay.
keyMissions: Faction missions involving Japanese and Western leaders
Replaying completed missions through Testament of the Soul
preparation: Upgrade two reliable weapon styles for different enemy matchups
Bring bonded allies into missions that allow companions
Review faction choices before replaying alternate outcomes
majorEncounters: Multi-enemy missions where ally switching can control pressure
unlocks: Testament of the Soul at the Longhouse

stages.storyFocus: Resolve the largest faction conflicts, face late-game duel chains and push the story toward its final outcomes.
keyMissions: The Mibu-dera choice between pro-Shogunate and anti-Shogunate paths
Late-story operations involving the Shinsengumi and opposing factions
Faction-dependent mission outcomes and ally availability
preparation: Finish important Bond Missions before advancing major faction events
Carry healing, status recovery and ranged options for longer missions
Use advantageous combat styles against each boss weapon
majorEncounters: Late-game faction bosses and consecutive duels
unlocks: Midnight difficulty after clearing the main story

stages.storyFocus: Complete character stories, Ally Missions, fugitive fights and hidden battles that sit outside the direct main route.
keyMissions: Bond Missions that deepen relationships and unlock rewards
Ally Missions built around established companions
Five additional Ally Missions added in the major May 2024 update
preparation: Raise character bonds to open more relationship content
Revisit regions after story milestones to find newly available missions
Use stealth and ranged attacks to thin groups before optional bosses
majorEncounters: William from Nioh as a hidden boss
Named fugitives with distinct weapons and combat styles
unlocks: Combat styles, weapons, items and dialogue rewards
Additional endgame practice and build testing

stages.storyFocus: Replay the campaign at the highest difficulty, rebuild Public Order and optimize endgame equipment.
keyMissions: Testament of the Soul missions with level sync
Alternate story choices and mission outcomes
preparation: Finish the main story to unlock Midnight
Build around Ki damage, set bonuses and reliable Countersparks
Use replayed missions to target missed rewards and alternate paths
majorEncounters: Restored Public Order encounters when deterioration is enabled
unlocks: Optional Public Order deterioration during Midnight world replay
Level sync selection for Testament missions

bondsRomanceStoryChoices.title: Rise of the Ronin Bonds, Romance and Story Choices
bondsRomanceStoryChoices.intro: Bonds connect exploration, combat progression and story choices. Character relationships can unlock combat styles, skills, weapons, items and dialogue, while region bonds reward players for restoring order and helping local residents.

bonds.title: Strengthen Relationships with Key Characters
howToProgress: Talk with visiting companions at the Longhouse
Give gifts that raise relationship progress
Complete Bond Missions and character-focused story content
Choose dialogue and story actions that support the character
rewards: More companion options in supported missions
bonds.notes: Central characters include figures from pro-Shogunate, anti-Shogunate and Western groups, so relationship progress can also affect how key missions are experienced.

bonds.title: Raise Bond with Yokohama, Edo and Kyoto Districts
howToProgress: Clear bandit-controlled areas and restore Public Order
Run errands for local residents
Defeat dangerous fugitives
Complete nearby side activities
rewards: More efficient access to local activities
bonds.notes: Region Bond is separate from individual character relationships and is raised through open-world activity.

bonds.title: Complete Companion Storylines
howToProgress: Advance the main story until a character mission becomes available
Raise the required relationship level
Return to the character or Longhouse to continue the mission chain
rewards: New allies or expanded mission options
bonds.notes: A major 2024 update added five Ally Missions and more dojo training partners.

bonds.title: Unlock Better Conversation Options
howToProgress: Invest in skill-tree upgrades that open special dialogue choices
Raise bonds to reveal additional personal conversations
Use dialogue options before or during mission events
bonds.notes: One demonstrated dialogue option allows the ronin to intimidate opponents and reduce their Ki before a fight begins.

bonds.title: Form a Special Relationship
howToProgress: Deepen the bond with an eligible character
Complete the character's relationship content
Choose the relationship option when it becomes available
bonds.notes: Veiled Vows are available only with selected characters, and the game allows the ronin to form more than one Veiled Vow.

bonds.title: Choose Factions and Character Outcomes
howToProgress: Make the first major decision after defeating Gonzo
Choose between pro-Shogunate and anti-Shogunate paths at Mibu-dera
Support different factions during key story missions
bonds.notes: Faction support can substantially change key missions and the people who fight beside the ronin.

bonds.title: Replay Choices and See Alternate Outcomes
howToProgress: Reach Edo to unlock Testament of the Soul
Access it from the Longhouse
Select a completed mission and choose a different path
bonds.notes: Testament of the Soul lets players explore other choices without restarting the full campaign.

multiplayerCoOpGuide.title: Rise of the Ronin Multiplayer and Co-op Guide
multiplayerCoOpGuide.intro: Rise of the Ronin supports online cooperative story missions for up to three players total. Co-op is mission-based rather than a persistent shared open world, and there is no PvP mode.

faqs.question: How many players can play Rise of the Ronin together?
faqs.answer: Online co-op in Rise of the Ronin supports up to three players total. A squad can include the host and up to two additional players.

faqs.question: How do I recruit players in Rise of the Ronin?
faqs.answer: Choose Recruit from a supported mission's cooperative setup. Set a password when you want the session limited to friends who know the same password.

faqs.question: How do I join a Rise of the Ronin co-op mission?
faqs.answer: Use Join or Search to find an available cooperative mission. Enter the matching password when joining a private friend session.
details: Search helps find a specific recruitment
Mission availability depends on supported story content

faqs.question: Which activities support direct co-op in Rise of the Ronin?
faqs.answer: Direct multiplayer is centered on supported story missions. General open-world roaming, map clearing and free exploration are handled in each player's solo world.
details: Supported story missions can be played together
Co-op is not a continuous shared-world mode
Open-world side activities are outside the direct co-op mission session

faqs.question: How does Rise of the Ronin co-op mission progress work?
faqs.answer: A co-op session covers the selected mission instance. When the mission ends, players leave that session and return to their own game, then recruit or join again for another supported mission.
details: Sessions are organized mission by mission
The party does not remain together for open-world travel
Testament of the Soul can be used to revisit completed missions

faqs.question: What is required to play Rise of the Ronin co-op on each platform?
faqs.answer: PS5 online co-op requires an active PlayStation Plus membership. The Steam version uses Steam online services and does not require PlayStation Plus.
details: Platform account and online access required

faqs.question: What are asynchronous ronin in Rise of the Ronin?
faqs.answer: Other players' ronin can appear in your world as asynchronous online characters. They represent other players without those players actively sharing your current session.
details: Can appear during normal world play
Separate from Join, Recruit and Search

faqs.question: How does the Rise of the Ronin Pilgrim Dog work?
faqs.answer: Deposit money with the Pilgrim Dog to send it into other players' games. It later returns with items for you, creating an indirect online exchange without forming a multiplayer party.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Rise of the Ronin

questions.question: What is Rise of the Ronin?
questions.answer: Rise of the Ronin is an open-world action RPG from Team NINJA set in Bakumatsu-era Japan, where you play as a customizable ronin mastering nine melee weapons, multiple combat styles and story-changing choices on PS5 and PC.

questions.question: Is Rise of the Ronin multiplayer or singleplayer?
questions.answer: Rise of the Ronin supports both solo play and online cooperative story missions for up to three players. There is no PvP mode, and the open world itself is not a shared persistent world.

questions.question: What platforms is Rise of the Ronin on?
questions.answer: Rise of the Ronin launched on PS5 on March 22, 2024 and released on Steam for PC on March 11, 2025.

questions.question: How many weapons are in Rise of the Ronin?
questions.answer: Rise of the Ronin features nine primary melee weapon categories, including the katana, odachi, spear and paired swords, plus unarmed combat, each with its own combat styles.

questions.question: How does the combat style system work in Rise of the Ronin?
questions.answer: Rise of the Ronin uses Ten, Chi and Jin combat styles that change a weapon's moveset and Counterspark. Matching your style to the enemy's weapon increases stagger and Ki pressure, and Shinobi styles reward perfectly timed Countersparks.

questions.question: Can you romance characters in Rise of the Ronin?
questions.answer: Yes. Rise of the Ronin includes character bonds, gifts, Bond Missions and Veiled Vow romance relationships with selected companions, and the game allows more than one Veiled Vow.

questions.question: Does Rise of the Ronin have multiple endings?
questions.answer: Yes. Rise of the Ronin features faction choices and major story decisions that change missions, allies and outcomes, and the Testament of the Soul system lets you replay missions to explore alternate paths.

questions.question: What are the Rise of the Ronin PC requirements?
questions.answer: Rise of the Ronin on PC requires Windows 10 or 11 64-bit, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 12 and 155 GB of SSD storage, with support for up to 8K resolution, 120 FPS, DLSS, FSR, XeSS and frame generation.

cta.title: Ready to Master Rise of the Ronin?
cta.description: Join the Rise of the Ronin community on Discord and Reddit for builds, weapons, combat styles, and PC performance tips!
